pattern: Track, as you scan the bits left to right, how many distinct subsequence-strings so far end in a 0 versus end in a 1, since any future count only ever depends on those two totals plus whether a lone "0" has ever been possible.
hints: A subsequence with a leading zero is only allowed when it is the single character "0" - everything else must start with a 1.

Sweep left to right keeping two running totals: distinct good subsequences seen so far ending in 0, and ending in 1. Appending the current character to every counted subsequence (plus, for a 1, starting a brand new one) updates these totals.

Appending a 0 only ever extends existing subsequences (never starts a new one, since that would be a leading zero); appending a 1 extends existing ones AND can start a new single-character subsequence. Add exactly one for a standalone "0" at the very end if the string contains a zero at all.

edge_inputs.description: all zeros collapse to one good subsequence
